item.title: Thousands of London Offices ‘Risk Obsolescence’ Under New Energy Efficiency Rules
item.description: Large swathes of London’s office property risk becoming stranded assets as landlords run out of time to embark on upgrades needed to meet new energy efficiency standards.

item.title: A Startup Confronts Water Shortages by Pulling It Out of the Air
item.description: As water risks are exposed by the Iran War and AI data centers, startup Atoco is preparing production of a device that can produce 4,000 liters daily.

item.title: Orange to Double Africa Solar-Powered Towers as War Boosts Costs
item.description: Orange SA plans to double its solar-powered base stations across Africa, as the fallout from the Iran war makes renewable-energy investments more attractive, its chief executive officer said.

item.title: Worst Start to Wildfire Season Raises Alarm as El Niño Threatens
item.description: Record-breaking heat and drought have fueled the world’s worst ever start to a wildfire year, as climate change and a developing El Niño threaten to push extreme weather to new heights.

item.title: IFM Warns It Will End A$3 Billion Fuel Project Without New Rules
item.description: IFM Investors Pty warned it may scrap a proposed A$3 billion ($2.2 billion) investment to make sustainable aviation fuel in Australia, unless the government mandates that airlines use the product.

item.title: New Zealand Plans Law Change to Block Action Against Polluters
item.description: New Zealand’s government plans a law change to prevent private legal action against greenhouse gas emitters, arguing cases have created business uncertainty and deterred investment.

item.title: Trump Taps Former FEMA Chief Hamilton to Lead Agency Again
item.description: The Trump administration selected Cameron Hamilton to lead the Federal Emergency Management Agency, which is grappling with reduced staffing levels ahead of hurricane season in the Atlantic.

item.title: Iran War Is Triggering a New China Push on Clean Tech Exports
item.description: Manufacturers of electric vehicles, batteries, and solar panels are wooing new markets.

item.title: A Strong El Niño? Insurers Are Grappling With Risk Uncertainty
item.description: The possibility of a super planet-heating event, which would make extreme weather impacts more likely, is challenging underwriters pricing climate risks.

item.title: EU Proposes Changes to Free Emission Permits to Appease Industry
item.description: The European Commission put forward updated benchmark values for its flagship carbon market in a bid to address concerns among some governments and manufacturers over the region’s declining competitiveness.

item.title: China Green Tech Firms Target New Consumers Hit by Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Suppliers of batteries, solar panels and electric cars are pushing to capitalize on rising demand.

item.title: Steel Sector’s Coal Reliance Puts Green Transition in Doubt: GEM
item.description: The global steel industry’s green transition is threatened by continued spending on coal-based production and underinvestment in cleaner methods, according to a clean energy research group.

item.title: How Polestar Plans to Make a Net-Zero Electric Car
item.description: The company is doubling down on making EVs greener.

item.title: Why Polestar Is Doubling Down on Net Zero as Rivals Pull Back on EVs
item.description: Climate ambition may be cooling, but regulations still require companies to measure and disclose emissions.

item.title: Budget Electric Cars Lure Europe’s Drivers After Fuel Price Jump
item.description: Demand for affordable electric vehicles is soaring in Europe as the fuel price increases caused by the Middle East conflict spur lower-income households to go green.

item.title: New York Prepares to Rollback Ambitious Climate Laws
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul cited soaring energy prices as a reason.

item.title: Salmon Farms on Land Take Aim at a $19 Billion Industry
item.description: They could upend the fishing sector—but only if companies can bring the costs down first.

item.title: Solar Firm Plans $750 Million Power Grids in Four Africa Nations
item.description: Renewvia Energy Corp., an operator of solar-powered mini-grids, is expanding into settlements in four African nations as efforts turn to renewable energy to plug a power deficit on the continent.

item.title: New York Readies Retreat From One of the Strictest Climate Laws
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul says the pullback is necessary to protect New Yorkers from soaring energy prices.

item.title: Trump FEMA Council Backs Sweeping Overhaul of Disaster Response
item.description: President Donald Trump’s task force for reforming the Federal Emergency Management Agency on Thursday recommended sweeping changes to how the federal government responds to natural disasters.

item.title: How a Suburban County Beat Back Sprawl
item.description: A study of US metro areas shows how the Bridgeport, Connecticut, region went from one of the most sprawling to one of the least.

item.title: Polish President Seeks Referendum on ‘Costly’ EU Climate Policy
item.description: Poland’s nationalist President Karol Nawrocki said he wanted to call a referendum on European Union climate policy in a move that’s bound to create fresh tensions with the pro-EU government and with Brussels.

item.title: China and Europe Form Carbon Alliance as US Bets on Fossil Fuels
item.description: China and the European Union have joined forces in a bid to create a global alliance on carbon pricing, putting them at odds with the Trump administration’s push to invest more in fossil fuels.

item.title: AI Push to Add $1.6 Billion to Maryland Power Bills, State Says
item.description: Maryland homeowners will pay an extra $1.6 billion on their electric bills over the next decade to subsidize grid costs to feed data centers, according to a state agency.

item.title: Chile Solar Panel Thefts Threaten Energy Infrastructure
item.description: The green technology has become more valuable as solar power goes mainstream.

item.title: A Quieter Hurricane Season This Year Could Still Pummel the US Power Grid
item.description: The number of storms isn’t the best predictor of damage to infrastructure or how long it takes to repair.

item.title: China’s Leapmotor Expands in Germany With EV Costing €49 a Month
item.description: China’s Leapmotor is targeting a bigger slice of Europe’s largest auto market with an electric vehicle costing less than a typical monthly mobile-phone bill.

item.title: Iran War Speeds Up the Trickiest Part of the Clean Energy Transition
item.description: Professor Emily Grubert joins the Zero podcast to discuss the difficulty of maintaining fossil-fuel supplies while clean energy is ramped up.

item.title: Mafias en Chile tienen un nuevo blanco: el robo de paneles solares
item.description: Con la rápida expansión de la energía solar en el país, ha surgido una sofisticada industria criminal de paneles y cables de cobre robados que alimenta mercados negros en distintas partes del mundo.

item.title: Thieves Are Stealing Chile’s Solar Panels and Cashing In on the Black Market
item.description: A solar energy boom in this narrow stretch of the Andes is marred by a wave of sophisticated theft as panels and associated copper cables disappear into black markets.
